title: Mutellina adonidifolia
intro: Mutellina adonidifolia, also known as Yarrow-wort or Buckthorn-leaved Yarrow, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a useful remedy for various conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems. Bioactive constituents of Mutellina adonidifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and to support immune system function.
taxonomy: Mutellina adonidifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Mutellina, which is a group of flowering plants native to Europe and Asia. The plant is also known as Adonis treleuki or Mutellina adonidifolia, although the name Mutellina adonidifolia is more widely accepted. It is a perennial herb that grows in dry, rocky places and is often found in mountainous regions. The exact classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the family Boraginaceae.
